meta.title: Business Continuity Plan — Claims Processing, Northgate Operations Centre
meta.subtitle: ISO 22301 BIA + recovery objectives (MTPD/RTO/RPO) + continuity strategies
meta.author: Business Continuity
meta.deid_notice: Recovery roles are referred to by role label. Any personal contact, home address or medical detail was scrubbed before drafting; the re-identification / contact key is held separately and is not part of this circulated document.

sections.text: Business continuity plan for the claims-processing function at the Northgate Operations Centre, grounded in ISO 22301:2019 (cl. 8.2.2 BIA, 8.2.3 risk assessment, 8.3 strategies, 8.4 plans, 8.5 exercise programme). The plan covers continuity of the time-critical activities after a disruption; the immediate incident response (muster, evacuation, scenario procedures) is owned by the emergency-response-plan and cross-referenced, not duplicated, here.
Critical activities, the impact of their loss over time, and their dependencies. The point at which impact becomes unacceptable is the MTPD; recovery objectives are derived strictly inside it.

rows: Regulatory SLA breach at ~24h; customer-detriment escalates after 48h
Claims IT platform; 1 case-management supplier (SINGLE); claims team
Hardship + reputational impact within 24h; FCA reportable after 72h
Payments gateway; Finance approver + deputy; banking partner
Complaint spike within 8h; trust erosion after 24h
sections.caption: BIA — critical activities, impact-over-time -> MTPD, and dependencies (single-points-of-failure flagged)
Every RTO is derived under a stated MTPD (RTO < MTPD) with a stated margin; every activity has an RPO (ISO 22301 8.2.2)

sections.text: Each strategy meets its activity's RTO/RPO and covers every stated dependency. Preventive controls are ranked by the hierarchy of controls — eliminate the single point of failure before merely mitigating it.

rows.action: Onboard a second qualified case-management supplier (covers the flagged single-supplier dependency for claims intake)
Stand up warm-standby for the claims IT platform + payments gateway to meet the 24h/48h RTOs
Cross-train a deputy Finance approver to remove the key-person dependency on the payment run
Pre-arrange contact-centre work-area recovery (alternate premises / remote) for the 8h RTO

rows: Run claims via the recovery strategy
Execute the payment run within RTO/RPO
sections.caption: Every recovery role has a named deputy — no single point of failure in the response itself (ISO 22301 8.4)
Exercise programme (ISO 22301 8.5) — owners + ISO dates (smart_actions); an untested plan is a [GAP]

sections.text: Next review: annual (2027-06-22) or on material change (new critical activity, supplier change, or a failed exercise). This BCP is decision-support and must be reviewed and signed off by a competent person; the SME (Business Continuity Manager, ISO 22301) review precedes — never replaces — that human sign-off.

For the immediate incident / emergency response (muster, evacuation, scenario response procedures, call-out tree, drills), see the emergency-response-plan — this BCP complements it and covers continuity of critical activities after the incident is controlled; the two are never merged.
